title: Note verbale ajoutant l'Australie à l'accession au traité

pages.text:
X84

PARIS.

8th February, 1939.

No. 90.

(361/2/39).

Monsieur le Ministre,

In accordance with instructions from His Majesty's

Principal secretary of State for Foreign Affairs, I have the honour, at the instance of His Majesty's Government in the

Commonwealth of Australia,

to notify their accession to the

Convention relating to the salvage of torpedoes, signed in

Paris on the 12th June, 1954, in accordance with the provisions of the additional protocol signed in Paris on the 12th

January, 1938.

2. I request that your Excellency will be so good is to notify all contracting partles accordingly, and to inform me in due course of the effective date of the accession.

I have the honour to be, with the highest consideration,

Monsieur le Ministre,

Your Excellency's most obedient, humble Servant,

His Excellency onsieur George Bonnet finister for Foreign Affairs
